papers.title_en: Learning Stable Canonical Worlds for Novel View Synthesis and Beyond
papers.authors: Xiaoyu Xu, Jian Zou, Sheyang Tang, Zhihua Wang, Jing Liao, Kede Ma
papers.evidence: 前馈高斯溅射用于新视角合成
papers.tldr: 现有前馈高斯泼溅方法依赖视图预测，多视图输入时易积累噪声，难以获得稳定场景表示。本文提出CanonicalGS，将多视图深度、语义和不确定性证据映射到规范潜在世界，通过不确定性感知融合聚合可靠信息。实验表明，新视角合成PSNR提升2.5 dB，语义分割准确率提升11%。该方法不仅提升了新视角合成质量，还增强了表示的可转移性，适用于下游视觉任务。
papers.abstract_en: Feed-forward Gaussian splatting (FFGS) facilitates real-time novel view synthesis, yet current methods often remain tied to view-dependent predictions. As more input views are added, they may accumulate noisy or redundant evidence instead of converging to a stable scene representation. In this paper, we introduce CanonicalGS, a feed-forward pipeline that maps cluttered multi-view observations into a stable, scene-centric representation. CanonicalGS first extracts view-centric evidence from depth, semantic features, and uncertainty estimates, and then aggregates this evidence in a canonical latent world using uncertainty-aware fusion. By emphasizing reliable observations while suppressing uncertain or redundant ones, CanonicalGS produces representations that scale more effectively for novel view synthesis and transfer to downstream visual perception tasks. Experiments show up to a $2.5$ dB improvement in peak signal-to-noise ratio for synthesizing novel views and an $11\%$ gain in semantic segmentation accuracy.

papers.title_en: PanoImager: Geometry-Guided Novel View Synthesis and Reconstruction from Sparse Panoramic Views
papers.evidence: 从稀疏全景图像进行几何引导的新视角合成
papers.tldr: 针对稀疏全景图在旋转主导弱视差场景下SfM/SLAM初始化失败的问题，提出PanoImager无SfM框架。通过前馈姿态/深度先验、几何条件扩散视图补全和深度引导3DGS优化，分解全景为局部视图并合成辅助观测，在极稀疏条件下实现稳定重建与跨视图一致性。实验验证了方法在极端稀疏下的鲁棒性，可作为地图细化的后台组件。
papers.abstract_en: Panoramic sensing offers wide field-of-view coverage, yet 3D reconstruction from sparse panoramas remains challenging under rotation-dominant, weak-parallax motion. In such regimes, SfM/SLAM initialization is often ill-conditioned and unreliable. We present PanoImager, an SfM-free framework that combines feed-forward pose/depth priors, geometry-conditioned diffusion view completion, and depth-guided 3DGS optimization. Given only a few panoramic images, PanoImager decomposes them into local perspective views, synthesizes auxiliary observations to enrich sparse evidence, and stabilizes Gaussian optimization for improved cross-view consistency. Experiments on multiple benchmarks show improved stability under extreme sparsity, suggesting PanoImager as an offline/background component for map refinement when SfM/SLAM fails to initialize.

papers.title_en: VolHuMe: a High-Resolution Large Scale Dataset of Volumetric Human Meshes
papers.authors: Giulia Martinelli, Niccolò Bisagno, Nicola Garau, Esa Rahtu, Nicola Conci
papers.evidence: 提供多视角RGB/深度图像和点云，用于三维人体重建
papers.tldr: 现有4D人体数据集多依赖全身图像，缺乏高分辨率细节。本文提出VolHuMe，使用64 RGB和32深度相机在容积工作室采集104名受试者，提供SMPL-X参数、高分辨率网格、多视图RGB-D、点云、服装分割及精细手脸几何。相比其他数据集，其近距离捕获保留身体细节，提升几何与纹理质量。在3D/4D重建任务上基准测试表明数据集质量高，同时揭示当前评估方法的不足。
papers.abstract_en: We introduce VolHuMe, a dataset of high-quality 4D human scans captured with a state-of-the-art volumetric studio using 64 RGB and 32 depth cameras. VolHuMe contains individual captures of 104 subjects and provides extensive ground truth, including SMPL-X, high-resolution meshes, multi-view RGB/depth images, rigged meshes, point clouds, garment segmentation, and detailed hand and facial geometry. Unlike prior datasets that primarily rely on full-body imagery, VolHuMe uses a close-range, high-resolution capture setup that preserves fine-grained body-part details, improving geometric fidelity and texture resolution. We benchmark VolHuMe on state-of-the-art methods across 3D and 4D human reconstruction tasks, showcasing the dataset's quality and exposing the limitations of current evaluation testbeds.

papers.title_en: GeoT2V-Bench: Benchmarking 3D Consistency in Text-to-Video Models via 3D Reconstruction
papers.evidence: 利用3D重建评估3D一致性的基准
papers.tldr: 针对相机提示的文本生成视频模型，现有评估仅关注视觉合理性，但忽略了多视图帧对静态3D场景的几何一致性。GeoT2V-Bench通过3D重建管道，估计相机参数并拟合可变形高斯泼溅，构建静态MedianGS代理，从而量化图像运动、渲染误差、流一致性及灵活与静态拟合差距。在12种模型和80个场景的3840次重建上，这些指标常相互矛盾，揭示了模型在全局场景获取中的多种失败模式，为3D一致性评估提供了新基准。
papers.abstract_en: Camera-prompted text-to-video (T2V) models are increasingly used to synthesize virtual camera captures, such as orbiting objects or moving through static scenes. For these outputs, visual plausibility is insufficient: the generated frames should also provide coherent multi-view evidence for a single static 3D scene. We introduce GeoT2V-Bench, a reconstruction-based diagnostic benchmark for evaluating whether camera-prompted T2V clips can support explicit rigid 3D reconstruction. Our pipeline estimates per-frame camera intrinsics and poses with VGGT-style geometry estimation, fits DeformableGS, derives a static MedianGS proxy by temporal-median aggregation, and renders this proxy along the estimated camera path. Instead of producing a pass/fail label or a single scalar score, GeoT2V-Bench reports a continuous reconstruction profile covering apparent image motion, estimated trajectory behavior, MedianGS static rendering error, static-render flow agreement, and the gap between flexible and static fits. On a fair-format four-seed evaluation with 3,840 completed reconstructions from 12 open-weight model configurations and 80 GeCo-Eval static-scene prompts, we find that visible motion, static rendering error, flow agreement, and flexible-vs-static behavior often disagree. GeoT2V-Bench therefore captures complementary failure modes that emerge when generated videos are tested as global static-scene acquisitions.

papers.title_en: Extracting Neural Materials from Multi-view Images
papers.authors: Kim Youwang, Jon Hasselgren, Peter Kocsis, Andrea Weidlich, Tae-Hyun Oh, Jacob Munkberg
papers.evidence: 从多视图图像通过逆渲染提取神经材质
papers.tldr: 神经材质能紧凑表示复杂高光反射，但获取困难。NeuMatEx提出结合大型材质重建模型LMRM预测初始材质与不确定性，再通过可微逆路径追踪优化。从多视角图像提取空间变化神经材质，视觉质量和材质分解优于传统PBR方法。该方法解决了神经材质潜空间非线性导致优化不收敛的问题。
papers.abstract_en: Neural materials can represent complex specular reflections and scattering effects in a compact, universal basis. However, acquiring and authoring such materials remains challenging. We present NeuMatEx, a differentiable inverse rendering method for extracting spatially varying neural materials from images. The nonlinear structure of neural material latent spaces makes optimization with naive inverse rendering infeasible. To address this, we train a Large Material Reconstruction Model (LMRM) that directly predicts initialbase color, neural material latents, and aleatoric uncertainty guides from images. This material prior provides a good initialization and better constrains our subsequent optimization using inverse path tracing. The predicted uncertainty further helps by anchoring high-confidence regions more tightly to the LMRM prediction, preventing lighting and complex specular effects from being baked into materials. Experiments on synthetic and real assets show that NeuMatEx extracts complex materials with better visual quality and material decomposition than PBR-based methods.
